title: Silky Sweet Uruguayan Dulce De Leche Pudding
description: A traditional Uruguayan dessert featuring caramel sauce, this silky sweet pudding is perfect for a lazy Sunday, with a sour and glassy-jelly texture, served in a scallop shell, it is also gluten-free friendly
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the sweetened condensed milk, egg yolks, granulated sugar, and melted butter, whisking until smooth.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat and stir in the vanilla extract and sea salt.
Strain the mixture into a clean bowl and let it cool to room temperature.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Just before serving, drizzle the caramel sauce over the top of the pudding.
Top with whipped heavy cream and serve in scallop shells.
Fry the scallop shells in a little bit of oil until crispy and golden brown, then fill with the pudding.
